text: This autofix commit successfully addresses 30+ accessibility, theme token consistency, and form UX issues across 25 frontend files. The majority of changes are correct — particularly the ProtectedRoute authorization fix (substring→exact match), keyboard navigation for dropdown menus, motion-safe animation prefixes, and focus-visible rings. Two regressions and a few missed spots remain.

reasoning: The spinner contrast regression on outline/ghost/icon Button variants blocks merge. Two critical and three important issues identified. Remaining fixes are straightforward and safe to apply automatically.

message: Corrected authorization check from substring match (rolesKey.includes) to exact match (allowedRoles.includes). The old code could grant elevated access — e.g. 'ADMIN' in 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN' returns true.
Comprehensive keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down/Home/End) added to the dropdown menu role='menu' — addresses WCAG 4.1.2.
Eliminated unsafe `as EventSeriesFormData` type assertion by defining a precise partial type for calculatePreviewDates.
Improved beforeunload handler to conditionally register based on form dirtiness instead of firing unconditionally on all navigation attempts.
Added motion-safe: prefix to transition classes on the shared Button component — respects prefers-reduced-motion globally.
Fixed install button focus ring from invisible white-on-white to visible brand-primary ring.
Loading spinner uses border-white/30 border-t-white for ALL variants, but outline/ghost/icon variants have non-white backgrounds — spinner is completely invisible on those variants at runtime.
suggestion: Use variant-aware spinner colors: for variants with white text (primary/cta/destructive) keep border-white, for outline/ghost/icon use border-current/30 border-t-current as before.

message: JSON.stringify comparison for form dirtiness is fragile — property order between data and defaultValues may differ leading to false positives. Also serialization is lost on non-serializable values (Dates, undefined).
suggestion: Use formState.isDirty from react-hook-form instead. It's the correct, performant API for detecting unsaved changes.

message: Frequency and day-of-week buttons still use transition-colors without the motion-safe: prefix, unlike the fixes applied to EventForm.tsx toggle switches.
suggestion: Add motion-safe: transition-colors to these button classes.

message: Same missing motion-safe: prefix on day-of-week button transition-colors as line 270.

The dark:text-brand-bg fix addresses earned checkmark contrast but dark-brand-bg is #0c1222 — black checkmark on dark bg may have insufficient contrast. Previously no dark-mode fix existed, so this is still an improvement. Verify WCAG 3:1 for the checkmark.
suggestion: Consider dark:text-white for the earned checkmark in dark mode to ensure contrast against the brand-primary background.
